steps.title: Follow the direction of influence
steps.content: The passage says Scottish dialects emphasize the “r” sound, Upland South dialects do the same, and the Upland South was colonized largely by people with Scottish ancestry. “Thus” asks for the conclusion drawn from those facts, not coincidence.

steps.title: Infer the source
steps.content: Choice B predicts future spread, which is unsupported. Choice C reverses the direction of influence. Choice D says Scottish settlers abandoned the “r” emphasis, but the Upland South still has it. The logical conclusion is that Upland South dialects acquired the feature from Scottish dialects. The answer is A.
